content.problemStatement:
You have 222 positive integers XXX and YYY.

In one operation, you can change the value of **one** of the integers by 111 or −1-1−1.

Find the minimum number of operations needed to ensure that either X≥(2⋅Y)X \ge (2 \cdot Y)X≥(2⋅Y) or Y≥(2⋅X)Y \ge (2 \cdot X)Y≥(2⋅X), i.e. either XXX is at least twice as large as YYY, or YYY is at least twice as large as XXX.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of test case contains 222 space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Y≥(2⋅X)Y \ge (2 \cdot X)Y≥(2⋅X) already holds as 4≥(2⋅2)4 \ge (2 \cdot 2)4≥(2⋅2).
Test case 222: Decrease YYY twice by 111 to get X=4,Y=2X = 4, Y = 2X=4,Y=2. Then, X≥(2⋅Y)X \geq (2\cdot Y)X≥(2⋅Y) holds.
This is the minimum possible number of operations.
Test case 333: Increase YYY by 111 to get X=3,Y=6X = 3, Y = 6X=3,Y=6. Then Y≥(2⋅X)Y \ge (2 \cdot X)Y≥(2⋅X) holds.
